question: What is the elevation of the city where the inventor of the telephone was born?

paragraphs.title: Alexander Graham Bell
paragraphs.text: Alexander Graham Bell, the inventor of the telephone, was born in Edinburgh in 1847.

paragraphs.title: Edinburgh
paragraphs.text: Edinburgh sits on a ridge and has an elevation of about 47 meters above sea level.

paragraphs.title: Thomas Edison
paragraphs.text: Thomas Edison developed the phonograph and the incandescent light bulb in Menlo Park.

paragraphs.title: Guglielmo Marconi
paragraphs.text: Guglielmo Marconi pioneered long distance radio transmission across the Atlantic.

question: In which country is the birthplace of the scientist who discovered polonium located?

paragraphs.title: Marie Curie
paragraphs.text: Marie Curie, who discovered polonium and radium, was born in Warsaw.

paragraphs.title: Warsaw
paragraphs.text: Warsaw is the capital and largest city, located in the country of Poland on the Vistula river.

paragraphs.title: Albert Einstein
paragraphs.text: Albert Einstein formulated the theory of relativity while living in Bern and Berlin.

paragraphs.title: Radioactivity
paragraphs.text: Radioactivity is the emission of particles from unstable atomic nuclei.

question: On which continent is the mountain range that contains the peak first summited in 1953?

paragraphs.title: Mount Everest
paragraphs.text: Mount Everest, first summited in 1953 by Hillary and Norgay, lies in the Himalaya range.

paragraphs.title: Himalaya
paragraphs.text: The Himalaya is a vast mountain range situated entirely on the continent of Asia.

paragraphs.title: Andes
paragraphs.text: The Andes is the longest continental mountain range, running along South America.

paragraphs.title: K2
paragraphs.text: K2 is the second highest mountain on Earth and is notoriously difficult to climb.
